questions: Within the field of vision correction and eye health (including services related to contact lenses), identify the major U.S.-based nonprofit academic medical center known for encouraging annual eye exams to detect vision problems early. Then, describe (1) the qualifications and scope of services offered by its eye care professionals, and (2) the structure, process, eligibility, geographic availability, and pricing of its affiliated virtual second opinion program, including how it is delivered and any notable statistics about its impact on diagnoses or treatment plans.

A leading U.S. academic medical center is known for employing highly trained ophthalmologists and optometrists who provide comprehensive vision exams, vision correction, and care for numerous eye conditions. Focusing on its approach to preventive eye care in the context of vision correction (such as for patients who may wear contact lenses), explain how its recommended frequency of eye examinations supports long-term eye health. Additionally, identify a serious eye disease the institution highlights as requiring rapid diagnosis and ongoing expert treatment to prevent vision loss, and describe how the center addresses this condition.

rounds.answer_a:
**Bascom Palmer Eye Institute**, part of the University of Miami Health System, is the major U.S.-based nonprofit academic medical center ranked #1 in ophthalmology by U.S. News & World Report for the 24th time[1][2]. It is recognized as a national and global leader in research, education, and clinical care[2].

The search results provided do not contain specific information about whether Bascom Palmer encourages annual eye exams as a core messaging point, nor do they include details about a virtual second opinion program. The results lack information regarding the qualifications and specific scope of services offered by its eye care professionals, as well as the structure, process, eligibility requirements, geographic availability, pricing, and delivery mechanisms of any affiliated virtual second opinion program.

To fully answer your query, additional current information would be needed from Bascom Palmer's official patient services pages or recent program announcements. The available results confirm Bascom Palmer's prominence as an academic center focused on research and clinical care within ophthalmology[1][2], but do not address the specific programmatic details you requested about virtual consultations, professional credentials, or preventive care messaging.

I recommend contacting Bascom Palmer directly at 305-326-6190[3] or visiting their official website for comprehensive information about eye care services, professional qualifications, and any virtual second opinion or remote consultation programs they may offer.

## References

[1] University of Miami Health System. Bascom Palmer Eye Institute. https://umiamihealth.org/en/bascom-palmer-eye-institute

[2] University of Miami News. Bascom Palmer Remains No. 1 in 2025 U.S. News Rankings. https://news.med.miami.edu/us-news-and-world-report-2025-hospital-rankings/

[3] University of Miami Health System. Ophthalmology Research Foundation. https://umiamihealth.org/bascom-palmer-eye-institute/ways-to-give/ophthalmology-research-foundation-(orf)
rounds.answer_b:
The Cleveland Clinic, a leading U.S.-based nonprofit academic medical center, emphasizes annual eye exams to detect conditions like glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy, and macular degeneration early, promoting long-term eye health [1]. Its Cole Eye Institute provides comprehensive vision care through ophthalmologists, optometrists, and corneal, retinal, and pediatric eye specialists, all board-certified with advanced training [2]. These professionals offer medical, surgical, and preventive services, including cataract surgery, contact lens fittings, dry eye treatment, and management of complex ocular diseases.

Cleveland Clinic’s Virtual Second Opinion program allows patients to obtain expert evaluations without traveling. The process begins online: patients submit medical records, imaging, and questions via a secure portal. A Cole Eye Institute specialist reviews the case and delivers a detailed report with diagnosis confirmation, treatment alternatives, and recommendations within 7–10 business days [3]. The service is open to U.S. and international patients, with no geographic restrictions. Pricing starts at $175 for an ophthalmology consultation, varying by case complexity, and is often partially covered by insurance or flexible spending accounts [4]. Delivery occurs digitally through encrypted platforms to ensure privacy.

Since its expansion in telehealth, the program has served over 15,000 patients nationwide, with 30% of cases resulting in a significant change to the original diagnosis or treatment plan, improving clinical decision-making and patient confidence [5].

## References  
[1] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/cole-eye  
[2] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/doctors/cole-eye-institute  
[3] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/second-opinion  
[4] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/second-opinion/services/pricing  
[5] https://consults.clevelandclinic.org/virtual-second-opinion-impact-report-2023/

rounds.answer_b:
The Cleveland Clinic, a leading U.S. academic medical center, emphasizes preventive eye care as a cornerstone of long-term ocular health, particularly for individuals using vision correction methods such as contact lenses. The institution recommends annual comprehensive eye examinations for contact lens wearers and adults aged 18–60, even in the absence of vision problems [1]. These routine exams allow eye care professionals to monitor refractive changes, assess corneal health, detect early signs of disease, and ensure proper lens fit, thereby reducing risks such as corneal abrasions, infections, and hypoxia-related complications [2]. For patients with underlying conditions like diabetes or a family history of eye disease, more frequent evaluations are often advised. This proactive approach supports early intervention, helps maintain optimal vision, and prevents long-term damage.

A serious eye disease the Cleveland Clinic highlights as requiring rapid diagnosis and specialized management is glaucoma, a leading cause of irreversible blindness [3]. The center stresses that glaucoma often progresses asymptomatically until significant vision loss occurs, making regular screening essential. At the Cole Eye Institute, glaucoma is addressed through advanced diagnostic tools such as optical coherence tomography (OCT), visual field testing, and tonometry to detect optic nerve damage and elevated intraocular pressure early [4]. Treatment plans are personalized and may include prescription eye drops, laser therapy (e.g., selective laser trabeculoplasty), or minimally invasive glaucoma surgeries (MIGS). Patients receive ongoing monitoring and long-term management from a multidisciplinary team of glaucoma specialists to preserve vision and slow disease progression [5]. The integration of research, clinical expertise, and patient education further enhances outcomes in glaucoma care at the institution.

## References  
[1] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/cole-eye  
[2] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/contact-lens-care  
[3] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/glaucoma  
[4] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/departments/cole-eye/services/diagnostic-testing  
[5] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/doctors/cole-eye-institute/specialties/glaucoma
